title: Configure and verify Layer 2 discovery protocols

questions.question: Which protocol is an IEEE standard that collects information from neighboring devices on their identity and capabilities?
questions.explanation: LLDP is IEEE 802.1AB and provides standards-based neighbor discovery.

correct.explanation: LLDP is IEEE 802.1AB and provides standards-based neighbor discovery.

incorrect.explanation: CDP misses what this question tests. LLDP is IEEE 802.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "CDP" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 802.1b uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 1 — not 802.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "802.1b" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 802.1a uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 1 — not 802.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "802.1a" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is DORA — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: You want to turn off CDP on a switch. Which command would you enter?
questions.explanation: no cdp run disables CDP globally.

correct.explanation: no cdp run disables CDP globally.

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#cdp disable misses what this question tests. no cdp run disables CDP globally.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#cdp disable" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config-if)#no cdp enable misses what this question tests. no cdp run disables CDP globally.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config-if)#no cdp enable" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#no cdp misses what this question tests. no cdp run disables CDP globally.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#no cdp" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: This stem tests cdp — exam distractors swap similar terms; anchor on: no cdp run disables CDP globally.

questions.question: You have several non-Cisco IP phones and you want to allow automatic power adjustments on the Cisco Power over an Ethernet (PoE) switch. Which command will allow you to achieve this?

questions.question:
CDP neighbor detail (SwitchB):
Device ID: SwitchB
Entry address(es): IP 192.168.1.2
Platform: cisco WS-C2960, Capabilities: Switch
Interface: GigabitEthernet0/2, Port ID (outgoing port): GigabitEthernet0/1
Holdtime: 162 sec

The following output shows the output of the CDP details. Which statement is correct about what is displayed?
choices: The advertisement was seen 162 seconds ago.
Switch B interface Gi0/1 connects to Switch A.
Switch B interface Gi0/2 connects to Switch A.
The IP address of Switch B is 192.168.1.1.
questions.explanation: CDP detail output identifies the local and remote interfaces, holdtime, and management address.

correct.explanation: CDP detail output identifies the local and remote interfaces, holdtime, and management address.

incorrect.explanation: The advertisement was seen 162 seconds ago. misses what this question tests. CDP detail output identifies the local and remote interfaces, holdtime, and management address.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The advertisement was seen 162 seconds a…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch B interface Gi0/1 connects to Switch A. misses what this question tests. CDP detail output identifies the local and remote interfaces, holdtime, and management address.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch B interface Gi0/1 connects to Swi…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The IP address of Switch B is 192.168.1.1. misses what this question tests. CDP detail output identifies the local and remote interfaces, holdtime, and management address.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Applying Layer 3 (IP) behavior to a Layer 2 switch process

answerReview.examTip: This stem tests cdp — exam distractors swap similar terms; anchor on: CDP detail output identifies the local and remote interfaces, holdtime, and management address.
